title: endolepis dioica
side_effects: Endolepis dioica can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach cramps. In severe cases, it can also cause respiratory problems, allergic reactions, and even kidney damage if consumed in large quantities. The plant's toxic compounds, known as alkaloids, can also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, leading to adverse reactions. If not used correctly, Endolepis dioica can have serious consequences for one's health, making it essential to consult a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
